 {"id":"gt-1py3y","title":"Unify gt sling and gt spawn with --args support","description":"## Summary

Complete the unification: `gt sling` becomes THE way to assign work. Remove top-level `gt spawn`.

## Current State (Partially Done)

- `gt sling` auto-spawns polecats when target is a rig
- `gt sling --args` stores args in bead (no-tmux mode, gt-vc3l4)
- `gt spawn` still exists with flags not yet in sling

## Remaining Work

### 1. Move spawn-only flags to sling

| Flag | Current | Action |
|------|---------|--------|
| `--naked` | spawn only | Move to sling |
| `--create` | spawn only | Move to sling |
| `--molecule` | spawn only | Move to sling |
| `--force` | spawn only | Move to sling |
| `--account` | spawn only | Move to sling |
| `--no-start` | spawn only | Evaluate if needed |

### 2. Remove top-level gt spawn

- Keep `gt spawn pending` as subcommand if still needed
- Remove `gt spawn` from root commands
- Update all docs and CLAUDE.md references

### 3. Unified UX Design

Target resolution (smart defaults):
```bash
gt sling gt-abc                    # Self (current agent)
gt sling gt-abc crew               # Crew worker in current rig  
gt sling gt-abc gastown            # Auto-spawn polecat in rig
gt sling gt-abc gastown/Toast      # Specific polecat
gt sling gt-abc mayor              # Mayor
```

Key flags:
```bash
gt sling gt-abc --args "patch release"   # Natural language context
gt sling gt-abc --naked                  # No-tmux mode (manual start)
gt sling gt-abc --create                 # Create polecat if missing
gt sling gt-abc --molecule mol-review    # Use specific workflow
```

### 4. Update Witness/Refinery/Deacon

These agents currently use `gt spawn`. Update to use `gt sling`:
- Witness: spawning polecats for work
- Refinery: spawning merge workers  
- Deacon: any spawn logic

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All spawn flags available in sling
- [ ] `gt spawn` removed (or hidden/deprecated)
- [ ] Witness uses `gt sling <rig>` for polecat work
- [ ] Docs updated
- [ ] Both human and agent UX is clean","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-26T13:35:55.902844-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.90011-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.90011-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}
 {"id":"gt-1qti","title":"Mayor restart loop: auto-prime and mail check on attach","description":"When mayor session cycles (after /exit, gt mayor attach reconnects), the new session lacks context.

## Current Behavior
- Mayor exits, shell script loop restarts claude
- New session starts cold - no gt prime, no mail check
- Mayor is disoriented, doesn't know prior context
- Strong 'antimemetic properties' - we discuss fixing it, then forget

## Expected Behavior
After restart, mayor should automatically:
1. Run gt prime (load role context)
2. Check gt mail inbox (find handoff messages)
3. Look for 🤝 HANDOFF messages from predecessor

## Possible Fixes
1. Startup hook in Claude Code settings that runs gt prime
2. CLAUDE.md instructions that say 'FIRST THING: run gt prime'
3. Shell script wrapper that injects context before attach
4. Modify gt mayor attach to inject prime command

- gt-sye: Mayor startup protocol prompting","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:44:43.188588-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.025195-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.025195-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-1su","title":"Spawn inject: Enter not submitted after multiline paste","description":"When gt spawn injects a multiline context (85+ lines), Claude Code shows 
'[Pasted text #1 +85 lines]' but the prompt is not submitted.

The tmux SendKeys function does include 'Enter' but it appears to not work
for long pasted text:

```go
func (t *Tmux) SendKeys(session, keys string) error {
    _, err := t.run("send-keys", "-t", session, keys, "Enter")
    return err
}
```

Possible fixes:
1. Use tmux paste-buffer instead of send-keys for long text
2. Send Enter separately after the text
3. Use bracketed paste mode correctly

Reproduction:
```bash
gt spawn gastown/Nux --issue gt-u1j.13 --create
# Session shows pasted text but waits at prompt
```","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:09:20.774203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-1xsah","title":"Add gt role command group with env-based role detection","description":"# gt role Command Group

Make role detection less fragile by using env var injection instead of cwd detection.

## Env Vars
- `GT_ROLE` - authoritative role name (mayor, witness, refinery, polecat, crew)
- `GT_ROLE_HOME` - canonical home directory for the role

## Detection Precedence
1. `$GT_ROLE` env var (authoritative)
2. Fall back to cwd-based detection (legacy/manual)
3. Warn if they disagree

## Subcommands
- `gt role` / `gt role show` - show current role
- `gt role home [ROLE]` - show home dir for role
- `gt role detect` - force cwd-based detection (debugging)
- `gt role list` - list all known roles
- `gt role env` - print export statements

## Integration Points
- gt prime - show role, warn on mismatch
- gt mol status - use gt role instead of own detection
- gt handoff - preserve GT_ROLE, reset to home
- All spawners - inject GT_ROLE and GT_ROLE_HOME

## Background
Mayor wandered to refinery dir, handoff preserved it, role detection broke.
Env var injection makes role stable across cd operations.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:50:53.378051-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-79jxg","title":"Bug: bd update --parent doesn't persist","description":"The --parent flag on bd update reports success but doesn't persist the parent field.

**Confirmed behavior:**
```bash
bd update gt-9kp3t --parent=gt-d0jqp --no-daemon -v
# Output: ✓ Updated issue: gt-9kp3t

bd show gt-9kp3t --json | jq '.[0].parent'
# Returns: null

grep gt-9kp3t .beads/issues.jsonl | head -1 | jq '.parent'
# Returns: null (field not present)
```

**Root cause hypothesis:**
The update handler processes the --parent flag but either:
1. Doesn't write it to the Issue struct
2. Doesn't include it in the JSONL export
3. The field name doesn't match what export expects

**Files to check:**
- cmd/bd/show.go:638-640 (flag handling)
- cmd/bd/show.go:810-814 (reparenting logic)
- internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go (RPC handler)
- internal/rpc/protocol.go (UpdateArgs.Parent field)

## Event format

```json
{
  "ts": "2025-12-28T10:30:15Z",
  "source": "gt",
  "type": "sling",
  "actor": "mayor",
  "payload": {"bead": "gt-xyz", "target": "gastown/Toast"},
  "visibility": "feed"
}
```

Visibility: `audit` | `feed` | `both`

## Implementation phases

1. **Event logging** - gt commands write to ~/gt/.events.jsonl
2. **Feed daemon** - goroutine in gt daemon tails events, writes curated feed
3. **Feed readers** - gt feed, gt dashboard read from .feed.jsonl
4. **AI curation** - future: haiku summarization for complex activity

 {"id":"gt-9wv0","title":"gt spawn should verify daemon is running for polecat triggering","description":"gt spawn notifies the Deacon (via mail) that a polecat was started, expecting the Deacon to trigger it once Claude is ready. But if the daemon isn't running, the mail sits unread and the polecat never gets triggered.

## Current Behavior
1. gt spawn starts polecat session
2. gt spawn sends POLECAT_STARTED to deacon/
3. (assumes daemon will trigger polecat)

## Problem
If gt daemon isn't running, step 3 never happens and polecat sits at prompt.

## Solution
In gt spawn, after session start:
1. Check if daemon is running (gt daemon status)
2. If not running, either:
   a. Start daemon: gt daemon start
   b. Or warn user: 'Daemon not running, polecat may not auto-start'

## Alternative
The user can manually trigger with gt nudge, but automated flow should work.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9wv0","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.187224-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"gt-eph-5jh","title":"Check Witness and Refinery health","description":"Check Witness and Refinery health for each rig.

**ZFC Principle**: You (Claude) make the judgment call about what is "stuck" or "unresponsive" - there are no hardcoded thresholds in Go. Read the signals, consider context, and decide.

For each rig, run:
```bash
gt witness status <rig>
gt refinery status <rig>
```

**Signals to assess:**

| Component | Healthy Signals | Concerning Signals |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|
| Witness | State: running, recent activity | State: not running, no heartbeat |
| Refinery | State: running, queue processing | Queue stuck, merge failures |

**Tracking unresponsive cycles:**

Maintain in your patrol state (persisted across cycles):
```
health_state:
  <rig>:
    witness:
      unresponsive_cycles: 0
      last_seen_healthy: <timestamp>
    refinery:
      unresponsive_cycles: 0
      last_seen_healthy: <timestamp>
```

**Decision matrix** (you decide the thresholds based on context):

| Cycles Unresponsive | Suggested Action |
|---------------------|------------------|
| 1-2 | Note it, check again next cycle |
| 3-4 | Attempt restart: gt witness restart <rig> |
| 5+ | Escalate to Mayor with context |

**Restart commands:**
```bash
gt witness restart <rig>
gt refinery restart <rig>
```

**Escalation:**
```bash
gt mail send mayor/ -s "Health: <rig> <component> unresponsive" \
  -m "Component has been unresponsive for N cycles. Restart attempts failed.
      Last healthy: <timestamp>
      Error signals: <details>"
```

Reset unresponsive_cycles to 0 when component responds normally.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T09:54:49.166307-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:21.319124-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:21.319124-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-eph-5jh","depends_on_id":"gt-eph-uqs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T09:54:49.251653-08:00","created_by":"deacon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-eph-6dn","title":"mol-deacon-patrol","description":"Mayor's daemon patrol loop.

The Deacon is the Mayor's background process that runs continuously, handling callbacks, monitoring rig health, and performing cleanup. Each patrol cycle runs these steps in sequence, then loops or exits.

## Second-Order Monitoring

Witnesses send WITNESS_PING messages to verify the Deacon is alive. This
prevents the "who watches the watchers" problem - if the Deacon dies,
Witnesses detect it and escalate to the Mayor.

The Deacon's agent bead last_activity timestamp is updated during each patrol
cycle. Witnesses check this timestamp to verify health.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:56:22.274106-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-28T15:56:30.255876-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-eph-8fu","title":"Nudge newly spawned polecats","description":"Nudge newly spawned polecats that are ready for input.

When polecats are spawned, their Claude session takes 10-20 seconds to initialize. The spawn command returns immediately without waiting. This step finds spawned polecats that are now ready and sends them a trigger to start working.

**ZFC-Compliant Observation** (AI observes AI):

```bash
# View pending spawns with captured terminal output
gt deacon pending
```

For each pending session, analyze the captured output:
- Look for Claude's prompt indicator "> " at the start of a line
- If prompt is visible, Claude is ready for input
- Make the judgment call yourself - you're the AI observer

For each ready polecat:
```bash
# 1. Trigger the polecat
gt nudge <session> "Begin."

# 2. Clear from pending list
gt deacon pending <session>
```

This triggers the UserPromptSubmit hook, which injects mail so the polecat sees its assignment.

**Bootstrap mode** (daemon-only, no AI available):
The daemon uses `gt deacon trigger-pending` with regex detection. This ZFC violation is acceptable during cold startup when no AI agent is running yet.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:41.718169-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:41.71817-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-eph-8fu","depends_on_id":"gt-eph-hfj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:41.727407-08:00","created_by":"deacon"},{"issue_id":"gt-eph-8fu","depends_on_id":"gt-eph-5c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T09:55:41.782453-08:00","created_by":"deacon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gaxo","title":"ZFC Cleanup: Move Go heuristics to Deacon molecule","description":"Remove Go code that makes workflow decisions. All health checking, staleness 
detection, nudging, and escalation belongs in the Deacon molecule where Claude 
executes it once per minute.

## The Problem

Go code currently implements polling-based health inference:
- Daemon watches heartbeats with hardcoded thresholds
- Decides if agents are "stuck" based on timestamps  
- Sends nudges, triggers restarts
- Parses mail subjects with regex to extract intent

This is a ZFC violation. Go should be message transport, not decision-maker.

## The Fix

Move all health/oversight logic to Deacon patrol molecule:
- Deacon runs once per minute
- Claude reads molecule steps, executes them
- Claude decides if agents are stuck (by checking mail, activity, etc.)
- Claude sends nudges/escalations via mail
- Go just routes messages

## Scope

1. daemon/daemon.go - heartbeat staleness logic
2. daemon/backoff.go - exponential backoff decisions
3. daemon/lifecycle.go - regex parsing of lifecycle intent
4. keepalive/keepalive.go - staleness thresholds
5. deacon/heartbeat.go - heartbeat age classification
6. doctor/stale_check.go - staleness detection
7. polecat/manager.go - state derivation from issues

## Success Criteria

- Go code has no hardcoded time.Duration for health decisions
- No regex parsing of mail subjects for intent
- No switch statements deciding agent state
- Deacon molecule has all oversight logic
- Go daemon is just a message router","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:58:18.684884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}
 {"id":"gt-gaxo.1","title":"Remove daemon heartbeat staleness logic","description":"**Files:** daemon/daemon.go (lines 272-325), daemon/backoff.go

**Current behavior:**
- Hardcoded 2min/5min thresholds classify agent health
- Exponential backoff decides nudge intervals
- Go decides "is this agent stuck?" based on timestamps

**Fix:**
- Remove staleness classification from Go
- Remove backoff algorithm
- Daemon becomes pure message router
- Deacon molecule step checks agent health instead

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.537382-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.537382-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-sadkq","title":"Execute registered plugins","description":"Execute registered plugins.

Scan ~/gt/plugins/ for plugin directories. Each plugin has a plugin.md with
YAML frontmatter defining its gate (when to run) and instructions (what to do).

See docs/deacon-plugins.md for full documentation.

Gate types:
- cooldown: Time since last run (e.g., 24h)
- cron: Schedule-based (e.g., "0 9 * * *")
- condition: Metric threshold (e.g., wisp count > 50)
- event: Trigger-based (e.g., startup, heartbeat)

For each plugin:
1. Read plugin.md frontmatter to check gate
2. Compare against state.json (last run, etc.)
3. If gate is open, execute the plugin

Plugins marked parallel: true can run concurrently using Task tool subagents.
Sequential plugins run one at a time in directory order.

Skip this step if ~/gt/plugins/ does not exist or is empty.

 {"id":"gt-y3jb9","title":"Protocol: Type System for Formulas","description":"Protocols define interfaces that formulas implement - a type system for workflows.

## Concept

```toml
# reviewable.protocol
[protocol]
name = "Reviewable"

[requires]
steps = ["review"]  # Must have a step tagged "review"
outputs = ["review_decision"]  # Must produce this

[guarantees]
"review completes before any deploy step"
```

Formulas declare: `implements = ["Reviewable", "Deployable"]`

System verifies compliance statically.

## Use Cases

1. Ensure all deploy formulas have review steps
2. Define compliance requirements as protocols
3. Enable formula discovery: "Give me all Deployable formulas"
4. Validate formula composition compatibility

## Properties

- Static verification (before execution)
- Composable (protocol can extend protocol)
- Documentation value (self-describing contracts)

## Open Questions

1. Protocol syntax - embedded in formula or separate files?
2. Verification timing - cook time? load time?
3. Protocol versioning - how do protocols evolve?
4. Inheritance - can protocols extend other protocols?

## Related

- docs/formula_evolution.md - "Formula Schemas" section
- gt-8tmz - molecule algebra
